page_article_reference: Autumn semester calendar rows by program/category
text: List autumn midterm exam ranges by program/category when no program is specified: Bachelor except Computer Science 17-22 November; Computer Science GEO/ENG 24-29 November; Master 17-22 November; One-cycle 1-6 December; first-year one-cycle English 5-10 January.
answer_policy: answer with exact calendar dates; handover if program/category unclear

page_article_reference: Bachelor regulation Article 19; study process diploma rules
text: Bachelor requires 240 ECTS; if one-cycle is relevant, Medicine is 360 ECTS and Dentistry is 300 ECTS.
answer_policy: answer exact credit numbers only from source

text: Suspension grounds include written request, foreign university study, illness, pregnancy/childbirth, military service, tuition non-payment, registration/document issues, and legal grounds. Maximum total suspension is 5 years.
answer_policy: answer generally; handover personal status cases

text: Course GPA = (X - 50) * 0.06 + 1. Summary GPA = sum(course GPA * credits) / total credits. FX and F count as 0.
answer_policy: answer exact formulas

text: FX is 41-50 points. The student needs more work and has the right to take an additional exam once.
answer_policy: answer exact FX rule; do not invent extra attempts

text: Computer Science spring academic/administrative registration is 9-14 March.
answer_policy: answer exact date

text: If final exam was missed for a valid reason and prerequisites are satisfied, the student may take an additional exam. It is after final results are announced, not earlier than 5 days.
answer_policy: answer conditionally; handover personal evidence cases

text: Final exam admission prerequisites include no financial debt and required minimum positive evaluations in mandatory/intermediate syllabus components. Do not invent additional conditions.
answer_policy: answer from regulation only

text: Master admission documents: ID card copy, CV/autobiography, one 3x4 color photo printed and electronic, military registration certificate copy for male applicants, notarized diploma copy, diploma supplement copy.
answer_policy: answer document list; handover personal eligibility cases

text: Admission without national exams is possible for legally defined categories, including foreign citizens/stateless persons with foreign full general education, Georgian citizens with last 2 years abroad, and other legal/ministry cases.
answer_policy: answer cautiously and cite law/ministry basis

text: Do not give one universal level. Regulation maps certificates to A2, B1, B2, C1; include B2/C1 examples if needed and recommend checking the specific program requirement.
answer_policy: avoid universal requirement; handover program-specific cases

text: Recognition is done by comparing prior completed courses/program with the Alte program. School and/or quality assurance evaluate compatibility; transcript, curriculum, syllabus and additional documents may be required.
answer_policy: do not guarantee exact recognition; handover transcript cases

page_article_reference: Master admission procedure and internal exam criteria
text: Procedure includes common master exam, university application/documents, internal university exam(s) in field and English and/or interview. Criteria include broad field knowledge, logical thinking, reasoned conclusions, speech culture, clear expression, exact and comprehensive answers.
answer_policy: answer general criteria; handover program-specific scoring

text: For bachelor administrative registration after national exams, proof of payment of half of the annual tuition fee is required. For other categories/programs, refer to contract/admissions office.
answer_policy: answer exact fraction for bachelor national exam case; handover other categories

text: University may require formal recognition, legalization and/or apostille. After university consent, applicant submits documents to NCEQE; after Ministry decision, university president issues enrollment order.
answer_policy: answer procedure generally; handover document validation

text: Partial: source confirms learning process organization involves educational process/student experience management and school, but full department goals/functions are not fully described. Additional official source/operator needed.
answer_policy: partial conservative answer only

text: University teaching language is Georgian; some programs are taught in English.
answer_policy: answer generally; program-specific language requires exact source

text: School/workgroup prepares program; Quality Management and Compliance Department reviews and gives expert conclusion; School Council discusses; Board of Directors makes final approval/refusal decision.
answer_policy: answer from approval process only

text: Uploaded official files do not clearly describe career development and alumni relations service. Additional official source or operator handover is needed.
answer_policy: do not invent; handover

text: Uploaded files do not clearly describe Teaching-Learning Excellence Center functions. Additional official source or operator handover is needed.
answer_policy: do not invent; handover
